utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , mrn49282721 . patient's name is jacqueline miller . use last visit exam where appropriate .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , how are you doing , jacqueline ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm pretty . good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good as well . so it sounds like we're , um , under good control right now .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . it's doing much better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good , good . do you have any rash leftover ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i have a- a small bit leftover . i started using , uh , doxycycline only one a day because i think the pharmacist said if it's getting better , to just limit it to once a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but i was taking it twice a day and i did notice a lotta improvement .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then when i started doing it only once a day , it seems the same and it's not continuing to get better . so it's kinda plateaued a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see . um , are you breastfeeding at this time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i'm not right now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . so you- you should not be breastfeeding while you're on that medication .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , actually i stopped breastfeeding and then asked for the doxycycline at that time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . even with the doxycycline , you can keep using the elidel .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and i did n't take the elidel because when i read about it i got worried . so i wanted to just try the doxycycline and see .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so we can talk about the elidel . a lot of the things you read about is not actually relevant to the cream or ointment form , but it's about the oral form that's used in really high doses , longterm , after people have a heart transplant or a kidney transplant or something like that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i did n't realize that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , and when you suppress the immune system that hard for that long , it can predispose you for developing cancers like hematologic type cancers . it does n't apply to as-needed use of the medicine , a cream or an ointment , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . then i can resume taking - taking the doxycycline twice a day again ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let me clarify . what exactly are you using ? and then we'll look at you and figure out , okay ? so you're using the sulfacetamide wash ? uh , how frequently are you using that one ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: twice a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and that's not drying you out too much ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you're using the metro cream ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how often are you using that one ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: after my face wash , i immediately apply the cream .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then , are you using any other kind of lotions or anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so you're not using the cetaphil cleanser ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm not .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and no neutrogena wipes ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no wipes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and then what about , um , any lotions that you're using ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm not using any lotion right now , just those two .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no lotion , okay . so that may be something else we should add in , a lotion to just help moisturize . but we'll see .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and then again , when did you go down to just taking the doxycycline once a day ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that was last week . so the first two weeks , i did twice a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and then i started seeing improvement , and then i changed to just once a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . let me take a look at your face here . i'm gon na describe for the transcriptionist what i'm seeing . you're fitzpatrick skin type iv , meaning you're not going to burn , you're going to sun tan , um , essentially , iv to v. and then on bilateral medial cheeks , there are a few really faint erythematous papules and just maybe a little bit of redness around and underneath your nostrils . so you're right , it's not totally gone .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i think i would go ahead and go back to twice a day , every day , with the doxycycline .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , but i would pickup the elidel too .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i can do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i mean , out of known risks associated with medications , topicals are usually safer and preferable to oral medications . with that being said , doxycycline is low risk .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it is ? okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and doxycycline can give you bad upset stomach or heartburn . um , it will make you sunburn , even if you never sunburn , so you have to protect yourself .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i mean , i feel sun sensitive whenever i go out , so i am taking all the precautions , with wearing a hat and all of that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . i just wanted to make sure you knew about that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i did . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . but i think to help get rid of it sooner rather than later , if insurance will cover the elidel , pick it up and start using it .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i did check . the insurance is not covering it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's not ? okay . well , let's look around really quick because if you use a goodrx coupon , it will be around 30 to $ 40 or something .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's see .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so with your coupon , it was around $ 850 or something .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: ugh , yeah . that's way too much .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i agree . and- and because i also looked at the eucrisa that you recommended in my notes , and if it's still ex- if it's still expensive , i could try that instead .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , so it ... actually ... it is actually more expensive . i think the prices fluctuate .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: because now it's showing the cheapest of $ 70 , and when i looked before , it was around 30 to $ 40 .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . if it was around $ 200 , i would've picked it up . but it was coming to around $ 850 after insurance .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so i've found the cream form . we could try ointment form .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you mean the tacrolimus ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so if we send it to pick'n save or metro market ... let's see where else .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: can you do the metro market ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . let me put in the prescription and we'll see what we can find .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: pharmacy is what i'm trying to say .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . but it's not a steroid , right ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: correct , it's not a steroid .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's called a calcineurin inhibitor . it's kinda like a steroid in that it calms inflammation .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but it's not a steroid , so do n't use steroids on your face , for sure , as they'll make this kind of rash worse . but also , steroids carry the risk of causing thinning of the skin .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: these medications do n't cause thinning of the skin and they're not going to cause some other kind of rash . um , the thing to know is that sometimes five to 10 minutes after you put it on , it can cause this weird kind of tingly or needle-like sensation or make it redder or flush . but it should only last a few seconds and then go away . it's not an allergy or anything bad .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so it only lasts a couple seconds . it does n't mean it's going to happen again . it's nothing bad . it will still work , so keep using it as long as you know you can stand it , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , thank you for explaining .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i will say , " apply to rash on face twice daily , until resolved . "

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . so only apply to the rash area ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , and keep using the face wash.

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i think you could just do it once a day . i do n't think you need to do it twice a day , just because i do n't wan na dry your sky out too much , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i never noticed that my face is dry or got thin . i feel so good after using it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i understand , but it could get dry and i do n't wan na create other problems for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right . i'll just wash once a day with it . but it was feeling very good , like there's this little bit of moisture getting back in . with the other wash , when i would use it twice a day , i was drying out . but not with this one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see . okay . well then do what feels good . if you do notice that you're starting to get dry , then reduce to once per day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so to review , um , continue using the same face wash , continue with metro cream , and increase the use of doxycycline to twice a day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then just do it until it's gone , and then do it for once a day for another week before stopping .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and i'll rewrite your instructions here .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you so much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: if there are any other questions or you're getting different instructions , feel free to reach out to me and we'll clarify , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but in general , doxycycline is such a low risk . we're not gon na have you on this forever . but especially since we're adding the tacrolimus , i expect it will clear quickly .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and i just wan na really help knock it out as fast as possible , since you've been dealing with it for so long now . i would take it twice a day until it's gone , and then once a day for a week before you stop . i'll write it out like that . so twice a day until rash is gone , and then once a day for another week before stopping .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: once the rash stops , you can stop the tacrolimus ointment . um , continue doxycycline another week and then stop . but you can keep using the wash and the metro cream .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good . i can do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then hold off until i see you again before you start back any of your old products . um , if it's starting to flair again , um , if it's just a little bit , you can try just doing the tacrolimus for a couple days , to see if that's enough . if it's not , then restart the dy- doxycycline as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do n't expect that to happen , but , you know , if it does , you have the tools . um , then you'll be seeing me for a followup . we can figure out what to do next . i would say if it's getting worse rather than better , just give me a call or send me a mychart message , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have enough refills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i think so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so it looks like you have another refill of the doxycycline , so you're good there . metro cream , you probably still have enough of that one .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and i'll cancel the elidel cream , just so we do n't complicate your list . uh , you have plenty of the face wash still .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . uh , and we'll get rid of the clindamycin from your list as well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and what would you suggest for a moisturizer ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , so for that i would do something really basic like vanicream . or even , if it's just really dry , you could use a little bit of petroleum jelly . um , i'll give you a couple samples , okay ? let's make sure we have a followup in about four weeks .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and i would stay away from any kind of anti-aging thing , any kind of plant thing . um , really let's just be sure we talk about any new products , or reach out to me if you're gon na try something else . um , let's go get those samples .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for assessment and plan , perioral dermatitis , rosacea , including ocular rosacea , and copy and paste her patient instructions . i recommend that she gently wash her eyelids once a day with gentle cleanser such as vanicream , also use bruder mask as needed .

detail_list.summary: The patient is Jacqueline Miller, a 40-year-old female presenting for a follow-up skin exam.
Perioral dermatitis
The doctor asks if the patient has any remaining rash.
The patient reports residual rash and that she reduced her doxycycline to once daily based on pharmacist advice.
The patient notes that her condition has plateaued since reducing the doxycycline frequency.
The doctor asks if the patient is currently breastfeeding.
The patient confirms she is not breastfeeding.
The patient did not start Elidel due to concerns about side effects.
The doctor asks about the frequency of the patient's sulfacetamide wash use.
The patient uses the sulfacetamide wash twice daily.
The doctor asks about the frequency of the patient's MetroCream use.
The patient applies MetroCream immediately after her face wash.
The doctor asks if the patient is using any other lotions or products.
The patient denies using other lotions, Cetaphil, or Neutrogena wipes.
Physical exam shows Fitzpatrick skin type 4-5, with faint erythematous papules on bilateral medial cheeks and redness around the nostrils.
The doctor instructs the patient to resume taking doxycycline twice daily.
The patient reports that insurance does not cover Elidel.
The doctor suggests trying Tacrolimus ointment instead of Elidel.
The patient asks if Tacrolimus is a steroid.
The doctor confirms Tacrolimus is not a steroid, but a calcineurin inhibitor.
The doctor instructs the patient to apply Tacrolimus to the rash twice daily until resolved.
The patient asks for a moisturizer recommendation.
The doctor recommends Vanicream or petroleum jelly for moisturizing and schedules a follow-up in four weeks.
Ocular rosacea
The doctor recommends washing eyelids daily with a gentle cleanser and using a Bruder mask as needed.
